english: du ról-ba (du rol ba) diverting himself with his wives; bdag-yód daṅ ról-ba (bdag yod dang rol ba) to divert one's self with a married woman (sensu obsc.) Schr.; in rgya-čer-ról-pa (rgya cher rol pa) (v. sub rgya (rgya)), and in ról-pa bkód-pa (rol pa bkod pa) (the n. of a certain kind of contemplation Gyatch.), it is used for ललित, playing. — 2. to take, taste, eat, drink, sriṅ-mo i śag-la ról-ba (sring mo'i shag la rol ba) witches or ogresses reveling in blood Mil.; ról-pai stábs-su bźugs (rol pa'i stabs su bzhugs) there he sits with greedy mien. — 3. = sprúl-ba (sprul ba) to practice sorcery, to cause to appear by magic power, rnám-par ról-pa (rnam par rol pa) = rnám-par sprúl-pa (rnam par sprul pa); yé-śes ról-pai kyeu lṅa (ye shes rol pa'i khye'u lnga) Pth. for: yé-śes-kyi sprúl-pa (ye shes kyi sprul pa) incarnations of the divine Wisdom; rol-pai mtso (rol pa'i mtsho) prob. enchanted lake, occurs in the description of the Sumeru, but no Lama seemed to know its exact meaning. — 4. vulg.: to thrash, to cudgel.
1. v. rol (rol) 3. — 2. col. for sgról-ma (sgrol ma).

(cf. ról-ba (rol ba) 1). 1. music, ról-mo byéd-pa (rol mo byed pa), W. *cò-če*, to make music, ról-mo spyád pa (rol mo spyad pa) Sch. id. — 2. musical instrument, = ról-moi ča-byád (rol mo'i cha byad) Dzl., ról-ča (rol cha) Cs., in W. esp. cymbal.

vapour, steam, ka-rláṅs (kha rlangs) breath, exhalation, *ka-laṅ táṅ-če* to breathe, to exhale W.; *gaṅ-laṅ* cloud-like snow-drifts on high hills, ču-rláṅs (chu rlangs) steam, watery vapour; rláṅs-ču ₒdón-pa (rlangs chu 'don pa) Schr. to distil.

1. moisture, humidity, rlan spáṅ-ba (rlan spang ba) to avoid the wet Med., rlan steṅ nyál-ba (rlan steng nyal ba) to sleep in the wet Lt. — 2. a liquid, rlan-rlón (rlan rlon) id., rlan-rlón čaṅ (rlan rlon chang) the liquid (called) beer Lex.; rlán-čan (rlan can) moist, wet, humid, e.g. a country, rlan-méd (rlan med) dry. Cf. rlón-pa (rlon pa), brlan (brlan).

wave, billow, flood, rgyá-mtsoi rlabs (rgya mtsho'i rlabs) Med.; ču-rlábs (chu rlabs) and dba-rlábs (dba' rlabs) or rba-rlábs (rba rlabs) = rlabs (rlabs); dus-rlabs (dus rlabs) ebb and flood, tides Stg.; rlabs gyó-ba (rlabs gyo ba) or ₒkrúg-pa ('khrug pa) the tumult of the waves Cs.; rlabs-po-čé (rlabs po che) or rlabs-čén (rlabs chen), Lex.: महोर्मि, a large wave or billow, a rolling swell of the sea, surf, surge; also fig.: a high degree, e.g. of diligence Thgy.
v. rlóm-pa (rlom pa); rlam-kyér (rlam khyer) Sch. pride (?).

resp. gsaṅ-rlíg (gsang rlig), testicle, stone, ₒbyín-pa ('byin pa), ₒpyíd-pa ('phyid pa), W. *tóṅ-če* to castrate, emasculate (a man), to cut or geld (an animal), rlíg-pyuṅ (rlig phyung), rlíg-méd (rlig med) castrated, emasculated, rlíg-čan (rlig can) having testicles, rlíg-gčíg-pa (rlig gcig pa) having only one testicle; rlíg-bu (rlig bu), rlíg-śúbs (rlig shubs) scrotum; rlíg-skráṅs (rlig skrangs) swollen testicles; rlig-rlúgs (rlig rlugs) Lt., rlig-blúgs (rlig blugs) S.g., id. (acc. to Cs.).
Sch. good, quick, cf. brliṅ-ba (brling ba).
Sch.: 'a whole, a lump or mass'; but this seems not applicable in the phrase dúd-groi rlíd-bu (dud 'gro'i rlid bu) Lex., and otherwise it is not known to me.

1. Cs.: 'to purge, mṅal rlúgs-par byéd-pa (mngal rlugs par byed pa) to cause an abortion, rlugs-byéd (rlugs byed) purging, procuring abortion; rlúgs-ma (rlugs ma) Sch.: 'the casting out, effusion'; acc. to one Lex. excretion of indigested food. — 2. Ts.: to overthrow, to pull down, v. lug-pa (lug pa).

1. W. rluṅ-po (rlung po) breeze, wind, rluṅ ló-ma-la reg (rlung lo ma la reg) the wind touches the leaves Dzl., rluṅ-gis skyod (rlung gis skyod) (a thing) is moved by the wind Dzl., blown away by the wind Glr.; *luṅ laṅ* C., *luṅ-po p'u dug* W., the wind blows, also for: there is a draught (here); *luṅ-rag máṅ-po yoṅ dug* W. one feels the wind (here) very much; rluṅ čén-po (rlung chen po) Mil., drág-po (drag po) a high wind, a gale; śár-rluṅ (shar rlung) east-wind etc., čar-rluṅ (char rlung) rain and wind; skám-rluṅ (skam rlung) a dry wind Cs.; *luṅ-po yób-če* W. to fan; ₒog-luṅ ('og rlung) wind (from the stomach), flatulence Lt.; fig.: lás-kyi rluṅ-gis déd-de (las kyi rlung gis ded de) impelled or pushed on by the wind of actions, i.e. involved in the consequences of one's actions; and in a similar manner in other instances, frq. — 2. air, atmospheric air, rluṅ-gyi dkyíl-ₒkór (rlung gyi dkyil 'khor) atmosphere; rluṅ-gi ₒprúl-ₒkór (rlung gi 'phrul 'khor) air-pump Cs., rluṅ-gi gru (rlung gi gru) air-balloon Cs. — 3. in physiology: one of the
